name: Apple Cheese Omelette
recipeIngredient: 1/2  Apple, peeled, cored, and thinly sliced
2 tablespoons Shredded Cheddar or Swiss cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. In an 8-inch nonstick skillet with sloping sides, melt butter over medium heat.
2. Add apple slices in one layer and cook for 2 minutes.
3. Sprinkle with 1 teaspoon sugar and cinnamon to taste. Cook until tender, about 3 to 5 more minutes.
4. In a small bowl, combine eggs, 1 teaspoon sugar, and water. Beat with a fork until the eggs are just blended.
5. Pour the egg mixture over the apples in the skillet.
6. Stir gently with a spatula to allow the egg mixture to cover the bottom of the pan.
7. Use the spatula to gently push the cooked portions toward the center of the skillet, tilting it to allow uncooked egg to flow into empty spaces.
8. When the egg is almost set on the surface, sprinkle cheese over half of the omelette.
9. Gently slide a spatula under the unfilled side, fold it over the filling, and transfer to a serving plate.
10. Sprinkle with cinnamon sugar and serve immediately.
TIP: The recipe can be doubled to make two servings. If more than two servings are needed, keep the first omelette warm and repeat the recipe. Serve this meal with a green salad, whole wheat rolls, and chocolate instant pudding prepared with milk.
